steps.title: Recognize the linear growth model
steps.content: The equation $0.5t + 6 = 10$ follows the structure: (starting value) + (rate) × (time) = (ending value). In any linear equation, the coefficient that multiplies the time variable is the rate of change. Here, 0.5 multiplies $t$, so 0.5 is the rate.

steps.title: Determine what 0.5 represents
steps.content: Hair grows from 6 inches to 10 inches, and $t$ is measured in months. The term $0.5t$ must yield inches of growth. For dimensional consistency: (0.5 inches/month) × ($t$ months) = inches. Therefore, 0.5 is the growth rate in inches per month. B is correct.

steps.title: Why the other choices are wrong
steps.content: A is wrong because it confuses the rate with its reciprocal — if the rate were in months per inch, it would be $\frac{1}{0.5} = 2$ months per inch, not 0.5. C is wrong because 10 is the value of $0.5t + 6$ when solved (the final hair length), not the coefficient of $t$; 10 describes an outcome, not a rate. D is wrong because multiplying 0.5 by 10 gives 5 inches — this misreads 10 as a number of months rather than as the target length, and the result has no meaning in the problem.
